'''片倉辺命'''（かたくらべのみこと）は、[[諏訪地方]]の民間伝承の[[神]]。
== 概要 == [[建御名方神]]の御子神で、[[守屋山]]南麓に坐す神とされ、また[[佐久郡]]片倉村にも鎮座するとされる<ref>諏訪氏系図.正編 十七項</ref>。
神陵は佐久郡にあるとされる<ref>諏訪氏系図.正編 十七項</ref>。
== 系譜 == 建御名方神と[[八坂刀売神]]の子で、后神は不明だが[[児玉彦命]]、[[上津御玉神]]、[[下津御玉神]]の三柱の御子神がいる<ref>諏訪氏系図.正編 十七、十八項</ref>。
その内児玉彦命は、[[千鹿頭神]]の後を継いで祭政を執り、守達神の娘・[[美都多麻比売神]]を娶って[[八櫛神]]を生む。 このため[[守矢氏]]の系図では四代目に数えられる。
== 祀る神社 == *[[諏訪大社]]上社前宮 若御子社 *諏訪大社下社秋宮 若宮社 *諏訪大社下社春宮 若宮社
== 参考文献 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == *[[建御名方神]] *[[八坂刀売神]] *[[児玉彦命]] *[[守矢氏]]
[[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 片倉辺命 | 神祇 = [[国津神]] | 全名 = 片倉辺命、片倉辺神 | 別名 = | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = 開拓の神 | 陵所 = 伝承あり | 父 = [[建御名方神]] | 母 = [[八坂刀売神]] | 配偶者 = | 子 = [[児玉彦命]]、[[上津御玉神]]、[[下津御玉神]] | 神社 = [[諏訪大社]]　等 | 関連氏族 = [[守矢氏]] 、守矢氏（副祝） }} '''片倉辺命'''（かたくらべのみこと）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪地方]]の民間伝承（[[諏訪信仰]]）の[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == '''片倉辺神'''、'''片倉辺尊'''とも表記され、また類似した神名に'''御倉辺命'''（みくらべのみこと）がいるが、同一神かどうかは不明。 [[建御名方神]]の御子神で、[[守屋山]]南麓（片倉）に坐す神とされ、また[[佐久郡]]旧入片倉村にも鎮座するとされる。神陵は佐久郡にあるとされる延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、17頁。。 明治初期に書かれた[[守矢氏]]の家系図『神長守矢氏系譜』の中で、守矢氏四代目として[[洩矢神]]の孫[[千鹿頭神]]の跡を継いだ者を、片倉辺命の子[[児玉彦命]]と記載する。また『諏訪氏系図.続編』においては[[片倉氏]]の祖神とされる。 == 系譜 == [[ファイル:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|サムネイル|{{Center|[[守矢氏]]の最初の五代
（洩矢神から八櫛神まで）}}]] 建御名方神と[[八坂刀売神]]の子で、后神は不明だが[[児玉彦命]]、[[上津御玉神]]、[[下津御玉神]]の三柱の御子神がいる諏訪氏系図.正編 十七、十八項。 その内児玉彦命は、[[千鹿頭神]]の後を継いで祭政を執り、[[守達神]]の娘・[[美都多麻比売神]]を娶って[[八櫛神]]を生む。 このため[[守矢氏]]の系図では四代目に数えられる。 == 祀る神社 == * [[諏訪大社]]上社前宮 若御子社（[[長野県]][[茅野市]]宮川） * [[十五社神社 (茅野市豊平)|十五社神社]]（長野県茅野市豊平） * 諏訪大社下社秋宮 若宮社（長野県[[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]） * 諏訪大社下社春宮 若宮社（長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町） * [[十五社神社 (岡谷市本町)|十五社神社]]（長野県[[岡谷市]]本町） == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[建御名方神]] *[[八坂刀売神]] *[[児玉彦命]] *[[守矢氏]] {{デフォルトソート:かたくらべのみこと}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 片倉辺命 | 神祇 = [[国津神]] | 全名 = 片倉辺命、片倉辺神 | 別名 = | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = 開拓の神 | 陵所 = 伝承あり | 父 = [[建御名方神]] | 母 = [[八坂刀売神]] | 配偶者 = | 子 = [[児玉彦命]]、[[上津御玉神]]、[[下津御玉神]] | 神社 = [[諏訪大社]]　等 | 関連氏族 = [[守矢氏]] 、守矢氏（副祝） }} '''片倉辺命'''（かたくらべのみこと）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪地方]]の民間伝承（[[諏訪信仰]]）の[[神 (神道)|神]]。 == 概要 == '''片倉辺神'''、'''片倉辺尊'''とも表記され、また類似した神名に'''御倉辺命'''（みくらべのみこと）がいるが、同一神かどうかは不明。 [[建御名方神]]の御子神で、[[守屋山]]南麓（片倉）に坐す神とされ、また[[佐久郡]]旧入片倉村にも鎮座するとされる。神陵は佐久郡にあるとされる延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、17頁。。 明治初期に書かれた[[守矢氏]]の家系図『神長守矢氏系譜』の中で、守矢氏四代目として[[洩矢神]]の孫[[千鹿頭神]]の跡を継いだ者を、片倉辺命の子[[児玉彦命]]と記載する。また『諏訪氏系図.続編』においては[[片倉氏]]の祖神とされる。 == 系譜 == [[ファイル:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|サムネイル|{{Center|[[守矢氏]]の最初の五代
（洩矢神から八櫛神まで）}}]] 建御名方神と[[八坂刀売神]]の子で、后神は不明だが[[児玉彦命]]、[[上津御玉神]]、[[下津御玉神]]の三柱の御子神がいる 延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、17、18頁。。 その内児玉彦命は、[[千鹿頭神]]の後を継いで祭政を執り、[[守達神]]の娘・[[美都多麻比売神]]を娶って[[八櫛神]]を生む。このため[[守矢氏]]の系図では児玉彦命を四代目に数えている。 == 祀る神社 == * [[諏訪大社]]上社前宮 若御子社（[[長野県]][[茅野市]]宮川） * [[十五社神社 (茅野市豊平)|十五社神社]]（長野県茅野市豊平） * 諏訪大社下社秋宮 若宮社（長野県[[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]） * 諏訪大社下社春宮 若宮社（長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町） * [[十五社神社 (岡谷市本町)|十五社神社]]（長野県[[岡谷市]]本町） == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[建御名方神]] *[[八坂刀売神]] *[[児玉彦命]] *[[守矢氏]] {{デフォルトソート:かたくらべのみこと}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]]

==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Dd]][[ja:片倉辺命]]

{{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto | japanese_name = 片倉辺命 | deity_of = [[Kunitsukami]] (Earthly Deity) | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe Kami | other_names = | titles = Deity of Cultivation and Development | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]] and others | texts = | parents = [[Takeminakata no Kami]] and [[Yasakatome no Kami]] | siblings = | consort = | children = [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]], [[Kamitsu Mitama no Kami]], [[Shimotsu Mitama no Kami]] | region = | associated_clans = [[Moriya Clan]], Moriya Clan (Deputy High Priest) | festivals = }} '''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa Region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], central to the [[Suwa Faith]]. [[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha Shrine at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]] == Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also referred to as '''Katakurabe Kami''' or '''Katakurabe Son'''. Another similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), appears in some records, but it is unclear if they represent the same deity. Katakurabe no Mikoto is regarded as a divine child of [[Takeminakata no Kami]] and is believed to reside in the southern foothills of [[Mount Moriya]] (Katakura). Some traditions also associate the deity with a shrine in the former Katakura village in [[Saku District]]. The burial site of the deity is said to be in Saku District延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、17頁。. In the genealogy of the [[Moriya Clan]] written during the early [[Meiji Period]], titled *The Genealogy of the Shinto High Priest Moriya Clan*, Katakurabe no Mikoto's child, [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]], is recorded as the fourth patriarch, succeeding [[Chikato no Kami]], the grandson of [[Moreya no Kami]]. The *Genealogy of the Suwa Clan* also describes Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the [[Katakura Clan]]. == Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|{{Center|First Five Generations of the [[Moriya Clan]]
(From Moreya no Kami to Yakushi no Kami)}}]] Katakurabe no Mikoto is the offspring of [[Takeminakata no Kami]] and [[Yasakatome no Kami]]. The consort of the deity is unknown, but they have three children: [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]], [[Kamitsu Mitama no Kami]], and [[Shimotsu Mitama no Kami]]延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、17、18頁。. Among these, [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]] succeeded [[Chikato no Kami]] as the spiritual leader and married [[Mitsutama Hime no Kami]], a daughter of [[Moritachi no Kami]]. Together, they had [[Yakushi no Kami]]. Thus, Kodama Hiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth patriarch in the genealogy of the Moriya Clan. == Shrines == * [[Suwa Taisha]] Kamisha Maemiya, Wakamiko-sha (Miyagawa, [[Chino City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Chino City Toyohira)|Jūgosha Shrine]] (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya, Wakamiya-sha (Shimosuwa Town, [[Suwa District]], Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya, Wakamiya-sha (Shimosuwa Town, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City Honmachi)|Jūgosha Shrine]] (Honmachi, [[Okaya City]], Nagano Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]] * [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]] * [[Moriya Clan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katakurabe no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Dd]][[ja:片倉辺命]]

{{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto | japanese_name = 片倉辺命 | deity_of = [[Kunitsukami]] (Earthly Deity) | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe Kami | other_names = | titles = Deity of Cultivation and Development | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]] and others | texts = | parents = [[Takeminakata no Kami]] and [[Yasakatome no Kami]] | siblings = | consort = | children = [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]], [[Kamitsu Mitama no Kami]], [[Shimotsu Mitama no Kami]] | region = | associated_clans = [[Moriya Clan]], Moriya Clan (Deputy High Priest) | festivals = }} '''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa Region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], central to the [[Suwa Faith]]. {{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto | japanese_name = 片倉辺命 | deity_of = [[Kunitsukami]] (Earthly Deity) | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe Kami | other_names = | titles = Deity of Cultivation and Development | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]] and others | texts = | parents = [[Takeminakata no Kami]] and [[Yasakatome no Kami]] | siblings = | consort = | children = [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]], [[Kamitsu Mitama no Kami]], [[Shimotsu Mitama no Kami]] | region = | associated_clans = [[Moriya Clan]], Moriya Clan (Deputy High Priest) | festivals = }} '''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa Region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], central to the [[Suwa Faith]]. revision.text:
{{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto | japanese_name = 片倉辺命 | deity_of = [[Kunitsukami]] (Earthly Deity) | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe Kami | other_names = | titles = Deity of Cultivation and Development | shrines = [[Suwa Taisha]] and others | texts = | parents = [[Takeminakata no Kami]] and [[Yasakatome no Kami]] | siblings = | consort = | children = [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]], [[Kamitsu Mitama no Kami]], [[Shimotsu Mitama no Kami]] | region = | associated_clans = [[Moriya Clan]], Moriya Clan (Deputy High Priest) | festivals = }} '''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk traditions of the [[Suwa Region]] in [[Nagano Prefecture]], central to the [[Suwa Faith]]. [[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha Shrine at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]] == Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also referred to as '''Katakurabe Kami''' or '''Katakurabe Son'''. Another similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), appears in some records, but it is unclear if they represent the same deity. Katakurabe no Mikoto is regarded as a divine child of [[Takeminakata no Kami]] and is believed to reside in the southern foothills of [[Mount Moriya]] (Katakura). Some traditions also associate the deity with a shrine in the former Katakura village in [[Saku District]]. The burial site of the deity is said to be in Saku District延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、17頁。. In the genealogy of the [[Moriya Clan]] written during the early [[Meiji Period]], titled *The Genealogy of the Shinto High Priest Moriya Clan*, Katakurabe no Mikoto's child, [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]], is recorded as the fourth patriarch, succeeding [[Chikato no Kami]], the grandson of [[Moreya no Kami]]. The *Genealogy of the Suwa Clan* also describes Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the [[Katakura Clan]]. == Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|{{Center|First Five Generations of the [[Moriya Clan]]
(From Moreya no Kami to Yakushi no Kami)}}]] Katakurabe no Mikoto is the offspring of [[Takeminakata no Kami]] and [[Yasakatome no Kami]]. The consort of the deity is unknown, but they have three children: [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]], [[Kamitsu Mitama no Kami]], and [[Shimotsu Mitama no Kami]]延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、17、18頁。. Among these, [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]] succeeded [[Chikato no Kami]] as the spiritual leader and married [[Mitsutama Hime no Kami]], a daughter of [[Moritachi no Kami]]. Together, they had [[Yakushi no Kami]]. Thus, Kodama Hiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth patriarch in the genealogy of the Moriya Clan. == Shrines == * [[Suwa Taisha]] Kamisha Maemiya, Wakamiko-sha (Miyagawa, [[Chino City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Chino City Toyohira)|Jūgosha Shrine]] (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya, Wakamiya-sha (Shimosuwa Town, [[Suwa District]], Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya, Wakamiya-sha (Shimosuwa Town, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City Honmachi)|Jūgosha Shrine]] (Honmachi, [[Okaya City]], Nagano Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]] * [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]] * [[Moriya Clan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katakurabe no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Katakura-hen-no-Mikoto]][[Category:pages from wikipedia]] [[ja:片倉辺命]]

In the genealogy of the [[Moriya Clan]] written during the early [[Meiji Period]], titled *The Genealogy of the Shinto High Priest Moriya Clan*, Katakurabe no Mikoto's child, [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]], is recorded as the fourth patriarch, succeeding [[Chikato no Kami]], the grandson of [[Moreya no Kami]]. Another similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), appears in some records, but it is unclear if they represent the same deity. Katakurabe no Mikoto is regarded as a divine child of [[Takeminakata no Kami]] and is believed to reside in the southern foothills of [[Mount Moriya]] (Katakura). Some traditions also associate the deity with a shrine in the former Katakura village in [[Saku District]]. The burial site of the deity is said to be in Saku District延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、17頁。. In the genealogy of the [[Moriya Clan]] written during the early [[Meiji Period]], titled *The Genealogy of the Shinto High Priest Moriya Clan*, Katakurabe no Mikoto's child, [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]], is recorded as the fourth patriarch, succeeding [[Chikato no Kami]], the grandson of [[Moreya no Kami]]. The *Genealogy of the Suwa Clan* also describes Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the [[Katakura Clan]]. == Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|{{Center|First Five Generations of the [[Moriya Clan]]
(From Moreya no Kami to Yakushi no Kami)}}]] Katakurabe no Mikoto is the offspring of [[Takeminakata no Kami]] and [[Yasakatome no Kami]]. The consort of the deity is unknown, but they have three children: [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]], [[Kamitsu Mitama no Kami]], and [[Shimotsu Mitama no Kami]]延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、17、18頁。. Among these, [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]] succeeded [[Chikato no Kami]] as the spiritual leader and married [[Mitsutama Hime no Kami]], a daughter of [[Moritachi no Kami]]. Together, they had [[Yakushi no Kami]]. Thus, Kodama Hiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth patriarch in the genealogy of the Moriya Clan. == Shrines == * [[Suwa Taisha]] Kamisha Maemiya, Wakamiko-sha (Miyagawa, [[Chino City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Chino City Toyohira)|Jūgosha Shrine]] (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya, Wakamiya-sha (Shimosuwa Town, [[Suwa District]], Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya, Wakamiya-sha (Shimosuwa Town, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City Honmachi)|Jūgosha Shrine]] (Honmachi, [[Okaya City]], Nagano Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]] * [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]] * [[Moriya Clan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katakurabe no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[ja:片倉辺命]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[en:Katakura-hen-no-Mikoto]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:片倉辺命]]

[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha Shrine at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]] == Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also referred to as '''Katakurabe Kami''' or '''Katakurabe Son'''. Another similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), appears in some records, but it is unclear if they represent the same deity. Katakurabe no Mikoto is regarded as a divine child of [[Takeminakata no Kami]] and is believed to reside in the southern foothills of [[Mount Moriya]] (Katakura). Some traditions also associate the deity with a shrine in the former Katakura village in [[Saku District]]. The burial site of the deity is said to be in Saku District延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、17頁。. In the genealogy of the [[Moriya Clan]] written during the early [[Meiji Period]], titled *The Genealogy of the Shinto High Priest Moriya Clan*, Katakurabe no Mikoto's child, [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]], is recorded as the fourth patriarch, succeeding [[Chikato no Kami]], the grandson of [[Moreya no Kami]]. The *Genealogy of the Suwa Clan* also describes Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the [[Katakura Clan]]. == Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|thumbnail|{{Center|First Five Generations of the [[Moriya Clan]]
(From Moreya no Kami to Yakushi no Kami)}}]] Katakurabe no Mikoto is the offspring of [[Takeminakata no Kami]] and [[Yasakatome no Kami]]. The consort of the deity is unknown, but they have three children: [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]], [[Kamitsu Mitama no Kami]], and [[Shimotsu Mitama no Kami]]延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、17、18頁。. Among these, [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]] succeeded [[Chikato no Kami]] as the spiritual leader and married [[Mitsutama Hime no Kami]], a daughter of [[Moritachi no Kami]]. Together, they had [[Yakushi no Kami]]. Thus, Kodama Hiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth patriarch in the genealogy of the Moriya Clan. == Shrines == * [[Suwa Taisha]] Kamisha Maemiya, Wakamiko-sha (Miyagawa, [[Chino City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]]) * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Chino City Toyohira)|Jūgosha Shrine]] (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya, Wakamiya-sha (Shimosuwa Town, [[Suwa District]], Nagano Prefecture) * Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya, Wakamiya-sha (Shimosuwa Town, Suwa District, Nagano Prefecture) * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City Honmachi)|Jūgosha Shrine]] (Honmachi, [[Okaya City]], Nagano Prefecture) == References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]] * [[Kodama Hiko no Mikoto]] * [[Moriya Clan]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katakurabe no Mikoto}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[ja:片倉辺命]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[en:Katakura-hen-no-Mikoto]][[en:Katakura-hen-no-Mikoto]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:片倉辺命]]

{{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto (片倉辺命) | divine_rank = {{ill|Kunitsu-kami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe no Kami | other_names = | alternate_names = | divine_rank = | divine_role = Deity of cultivation | burial = Said to be in {{ill|Saku District|ja|佐久郡}} | father = {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} | mother = {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神}}, {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神}} | shrines = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} and others | related_clans = {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, Moriya (Vice-Priest) }}
'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]
== Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.
Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}
According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Katakura clan|ja|片倉氏}}.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}]]
Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}.
== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto == * {{ill|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮若御子社}} (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)|ja|十五社神社}} (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮若宮社}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja|諏訪大社下社春宮若宮社}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgoshajinja (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社}} (Honcho, Okaya City, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}} * {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}} * {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}

{{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto (片倉辺命) | divine_rank = {{ill|Kunitsu-kami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe no Kami | other_names = | alternate_names = | divine_rank = | divine_role = Deity of cultivation | burial = Said to be in {{ill|Saku District|ja|佐久郡}} | father = {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} | mother = {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神}}, {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神}} | shrines = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} and others | related_clans = {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, Moriya (Vice-Priest) }}
'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]
== Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.
Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}
According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Katakura clan|ja|片倉氏}}.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}]]
Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}.
== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto == * {{ill|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮若御子社}} (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)|ja|十五社神社}} (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮若宮社}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja|諏訪大社下社春宮若宮社}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgoshajinja (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社}} (Honcho, Okaya City, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}} * {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}} * {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}

For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}.

{{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto (片倉辺命) | divine_rank = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe no Kami | other_names = | alternate_names = | divine_rank = | divine_role = Deity of cultivation | burial = Said to be in {{ill|Saku District|ja|佐久郡}} | father = {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} | mother = {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神}}, {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神}} | shrines = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}} and others | related_clans = {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, Moriya (Vice-Priest) }}
'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]
== Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.
Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}
According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Katakura clan|ja|片倉氏}}.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}]]
Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}.

== Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.
Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}
According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Katakura clan|ja|片倉氏}}.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}]]
Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}.
== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto == * {{ill|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮若御子社}} (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)|ja|十五社神社}} (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮若宮社}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja|諏訪大社下社春宮若宮社}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgoshajinja (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社}} (Honcho, Okaya City, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}} * {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}} * {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}

{{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto (片倉辺命) | divine_rank = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe no Kami | other_names = | alternate_names = | divine_rank = | divine_role = Deity of cultivation | burial = Said to be in {{ill|Saku District|ja|佐久郡}} | father = {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} | mother = {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神}}, {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神}} | shrines = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} and others | related_clans = {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, Moriya (Vice-Priest) }}
'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]
== Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.
Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}
According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Katakura clan|ja|片倉氏}}.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}]]
Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}.
== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto == * {{ill|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮若御子社}} (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)|ja|十五社神社}} (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮若宮社}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja|諏訪大社下社春宮若宮社}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgoshajinja (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社}} (Honcho, Okaya City, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}} * {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}} * {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}

{{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto (片倉辺命) | divine_rank = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe no Kami | other_names = | alternate_names = | divine_rank = | divine_role = Deity of cultivation | burial = Said to be in {{ill|Saku District|ja|佐久郡}} | father = {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} | mother = {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神}}, {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神}} | shrines = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} and others | related_clans = {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}, Moriya (Vice-Priest) }}
'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]
== Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.
Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}
According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Katakura clan|ja|片倉氏}}.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}]]
Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}.

{{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto (片倉辺命) | divine_rank = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe no Kami | other_names = | alternate_names = | divine_rank = | divine_role = Deity of cultivation | burial = Said to be in {{ill|Saku District|ja|佐久郡}} | father = {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} | mother = {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}} | spouse = | children = {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神}}, {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神}} | shrines = {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}} and others | related_clans = {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}, Moriya (Vice-Priest) }}
'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰}}.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]
== Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.
Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}
According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Katakura clan|ja|片倉氏}}.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}]]
Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}.
== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto == * {{ill|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮若御子社}} (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)|ja|十五社神社}} (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮若宮社}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja|諏訪大社下社春宮若宮社}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgoshajinja (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社}} (Honcho, Okaya City, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}} * {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}} * {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}

[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]
== Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.
Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}}, residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}
According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Katakura clan|ja|片倉氏}}.
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}]]
Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} and {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}}. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}.
== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto == * {{ill|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮若御子社}} (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)|ja|十五社神社}} (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮若宮社}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja|諏訪大社下社春宮若宮社}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgoshajinja (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社}} (Honcho, Okaya City, Nagano Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Kami|ja|建御名方神}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Kami|ja|八坂刀売神}} * {{ill|Kodamahiko no Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命}} * {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan}}

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 片倉辺命 | 神祇 = [[国津神]] | 全名 = 片倉辺命、片倉辺神 | 別名 = | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = 開拓の神 | 陵所 = 伝承あり | 父 = [[建御名方神]] | 母 = [[八坂刀売神]] | 配偶者 = | 子 = [[児玉彦命]]、[[上津御玉神]]、[[下津御玉神]] | 神社 = [[諏訪大社]]　等 | 関連氏族 = [[守矢氏]] 、守矢氏（副祝） }} '''片倉辺命'''（かたくらべのみこと）は、[[長野県]][[諏訪地方]]の民間伝承（[[諏訪信仰]]）の[[神 (神道)|神]]。 [[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|諏訪大社前宮若御子社]] == 概要 == '''片倉辺神'''、'''片倉辺尊'''とも表記され、また類似した神名に'''御倉辺命'''（みくらべのみこと）がいるが、同一神かどうかは不明。 [[建御名方神]]の御子神で、[[守屋山]]南麓（片倉）に坐す神とされ、また[[佐久郡]]旧入片倉村にも鎮座するとされる。神陵は佐久郡にあるとされる延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、17頁。。 明治初期に書かれた[[守矢氏]]の家系図『神長守矢氏系譜』の中で、守矢氏四代目として[[洩矢神]]の孫[[千鹿頭神]]の跡を継いだ者を、片倉辺命の子[[児玉彦命]]と記載する。また『諏訪氏系図.続編』においては[[片倉氏]]の祖神とされる。 == 系譜 == [[ファイル:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|170px|サムネイル|{{Center|[[守矢氏]]の最初の五代
（洩矢神から八櫛神まで）}}]] 建御名方神と[[八坂刀売神]]の子で、后神は不明だが[[児玉彦命]]、[[上津御玉神]]、[[下津御玉神]]の三柱の御子神がいる 延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、17、18頁。。 その内児玉彦命は、[[千鹿頭神]]の後を継いで祭政を執り、[[守達神]]の娘・[[美都多麻比売神]]を娶って[[八櫛神]]を生む。このため[[守矢氏]]の系図では児玉彦命を四代目に数えている。 == 祀る神社 == * [[諏訪大社]]上社前宮 若御子社（[[長野県]][[茅野市]]宮川） * [[十五社神社 (茅野市豊平)|十五社神社]]（長野県茅野市豊平） * 諏訪大社下社秋宮 若宮社（長野県[[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]） * 諏訪大社下社春宮 若宮社（長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町） * [[十五社神社 (岡谷市本町)|十五社神社]]（長野県[[岡谷市]]本町） == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[建御名方神]] *[[八坂刀売神]] *[[児玉彦命]] *[[守矢氏]] {{デフォルトソート:かたくらべのみこと}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]]

（洩矢神から八櫛神まで）}}]] 建御名方神と[[八坂刀売神]]の子で、后神は不明だが[[児玉彦命]]、[[上津御玉神]]、[[下津御玉神]]の三柱の御子神がいる 延川和彦「修補諏訪氏系図」『諏訪氏系図.正編』飯田好太郎、大正10年、17、18頁。。 その内児玉彦命は、[[千鹿頭神]]の後を継いで祭政を執り、[[守達神]]の娘・[[美都多麻比売神]]を娶って[[八櫛神]]を生む。このため[[守矢氏]]の系図では児玉彦命を四代目に数えている。 == 祀る神社 == * [[諏訪大社]]上社前宮 若御子社（[[長野県]][[茅野市]]宮川） * [[十五社神社 (茅野市豊平)|十五社神社]]（長野県茅野市豊平） * 諏訪大社下社秋宮 若宮社（長野県[[諏訪郡]][[下諏訪町]]） * 諏訪大社下社春宮 若宮社（長野県諏訪郡下諏訪町） * [[十五社神社 (岡谷市本町)|十五社神社]]（長野県[[岡谷市]]本町） == 参考文献 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[建御名方神]] *[[八坂刀売神]] *[[児玉彦命]] *[[守矢氏]] {{デフォルトソート:かたくらべのみこと}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片倉辺命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q60995918]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q60995918]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto (片倉辺命) | divine_Rank = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe no Kami | other_names = | alternate_names = | divine_Rank = | divine_role = Deity of cultivation | burial = Said to be in [[:en:Saku District, Shinano|Saku District]] | father = [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] | mother = [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]] | spouse = | children = {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | shrines = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] and others | related_clans = {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, Moriya (Vice-Priest) }}
'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}}.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]
== Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.
Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyukatakura Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}
According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Katakura clan|Katakura clan]].
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片倉辺命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q60995918]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q60995918]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto (片倉辺命) | divine_Rank = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe no Kami | other_names = | alternate_names = | divine_Rank = | divine_role = Deity of cultivation | burial = Said to be in [[:en:Saku District, Shinano|Saku District]] | father = [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] | mother = [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]] | spouse = | children = {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | shrines = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] and others | related_clans = {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, Moriya (Vice-Priest) }}
'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa faith|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}}.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]
== Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.
Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyukatakura Village|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}
According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Katakura clan|Katakura clan]].
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}.
== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto == * {{ill|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮若御子社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579687}} (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)|qq=}} (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮若宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579688}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja|諏訪大社下社春宮若宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579689}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Okaya City)|qq=}} (Honcho, Okaya City, Nagano Prefecture)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片倉辺命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60995918]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60995918]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto (片倉辺命) | divine_Rank = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe no Kami | other_names = | alternate_names = | divine_Rank = | divine_role = Deity of cultivation | burial = Said to be in [[:en:Saku District, Shinano|Saku District]] | father = [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] | mother = [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]] | spouse = | children = {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | shrines = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] and others | related_clans = {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, Moriya (Vice-Priest) }}
'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}}.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]
== Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.
Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyukatakura Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}
According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Katakura clan|Katakura clan]].
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}.
== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto == * {{ill|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮若御子社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579687}} (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)|qq=}} (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮若宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579688}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja|諏訪大社下社春宮若宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579689}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Okaya City)|qq=}} (Honcho, Okaya City, Nagano Prefecture)

Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}.
== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto == * {{ill|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮若御子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579687}} (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)|qq=}} (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579688}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja|諏訪大社下社春宮若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579689}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Okaya City)|qq=}} (Honcho, Okaya City, Nagano Prefecture)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片倉辺命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60995918]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60995918]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto (片倉辺命) | divine_Rank = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe no Kami | other_names = | alternate_names = | divine_Rank = | divine_role = Deity of cultivation | burial = Said to be in [[:en:Saku District, Shinano|Saku District]] | father = [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] | mother = [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]] | spouse = | children = {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | shrines = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] and others | related_clans = {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, Moriya (Vice-Priest) }}
'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}}.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]
== Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.
Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyukatakura Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}
According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Katakura clan|Katakura clan]].
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}.
== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto == * {{ill|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮若御子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579687}} (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)|qq=}} (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579688}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja|諏訪大社下社春宮若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579689}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Okaya City)|qq=}} (Honcho, Okaya City, Nagano Prefecture)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片倉辺命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60995918]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60995918]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto (片倉辺命) | divine_Rank = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe no Kami | other_names = | alternate_names = | divine_Rank = | divine_role = Deity of cultivation | burial = Said to be in [[:en:Saku District, Shinano|Saku District]] | father = [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] | mother = [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]] | spouse = | children = {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | shrines = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] and others | related_clans = {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, Moriya (Vice-Priest) }}
'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}}.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]
== Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.
Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyukatakura Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}
According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Katakura clan|Katakura clan]].
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}.
== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto == * {{ill|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮若御子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579687}} (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)|qq=}} (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579688}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja|諏訪大社下社春宮若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579689}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Okaya City)|qq=}} (Honcho, Okaya City, Nagano Prefecture)

Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}.
