<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片倉辺命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60995918]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60995918]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto (片倉辺命) | divine_Rank = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe no Kami | other_names = | alternate_names = | divine_Rank = | divine_role = Deity of cultivation | burial = Said to be in [[:en:Saku District, Shinano|Saku District]] | father = [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] | mother = [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]] | spouse = | children = {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | shrines = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] and others | related_clans = {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, Moriya (Vice-Priest) }}
'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}}.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]
== Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.
Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyukatakura Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}
According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Katakura clan|Katakura clan]].
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}.
== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto == * {{ill|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮若御子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579687}} (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)|qq=}} (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579688}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja|諏訪大社下社春宮若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579689}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Okaya City)|qq=}} (Honcho, Okaya City, Nagano Prefecture)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片倉辺命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片倉辺命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60995918]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60995918]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto (片倉辺命) | divine_Rank = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe no Kami | other_names = | alternate_names = | divine_Rank = | divine_role = Deity of cultivation | burial = Said to be in [[:en:Saku District, Shinano|Saku District]] | father = [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] | mother = [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]] | spouse = | children = {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | shrines = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] and others | related_clans = {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, Moriya (Vice-Priest) }}
'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}}.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]
== Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.
Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyukatakura Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}
According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Katakura clan|Katakura clan]].
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}.
== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto == * {{ill|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮若御子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579687}} (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)|qq=}} (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579688}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja|諏訪大社下社春宮若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579689}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Okaya City)|qq=}} (Honcho, Okaya City, Nagano Prefecture)

Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片倉辺命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片倉辺命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片倉辺命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片倉辺命]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60995918]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60995918]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity | name = Katakurabe no Mikoto (片倉辺命) | divine_Rank = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full_name = Katakurabe no Mikoto, Katakurabe no Kami | other_names = | alternate_names = | divine_Rank = | divine_role = Deity of cultivation | burial = Said to be in [[:en:Saku District, Shinano|Saku District]] | father = [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] | mother = [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]] | spouse = | children = {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | shrines = [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]] and others | related_clans = {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, Moriya (Vice-Priest) }}
'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}}.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]
== Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.
Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyukatakura Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}
According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Katakura clan|Katakura clan]].
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}.
== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto == * {{ill|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮若御子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579687}} (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)|qq=}} (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579688}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja|諏訪大社下社春宮若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579689}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Okaya City)|qq=}} (Honcho, Okaya City, Nagano Prefecture)

'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}}.
[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]
== Overview == Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.
Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyukatakura Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}
According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Katakura clan|Katakura clan]].
== Genealogy == [[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]
Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]] and [[:en:Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}
Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}.
== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto == * {{ill|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮若御子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579687}} (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)|qq=}} (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579688}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja|諏訪大社下社春宮若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579689}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture) * {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Okaya City)|qq=}} (Honcho, Okaya City, Nagano Prefecture)

'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}}.

[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]

== Overview ==
Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.

Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}, residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyukatakura Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}

According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Katakura clan|en|Katakura clan|WD=Q15149847|lt=Katakura clan}}.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome no Kami}}. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}

Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}.

== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto ==
* {{ill|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮若御子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579687}} (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture)

== Overview ==
Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.

The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}

According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Katakura clan|en|Katakura clan|WD=Q15149847|lt=Katakura clan}}.

While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}

'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}}.

[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]

== Overview ==
Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.

Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}, residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyukatakura Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}

According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Katakura clan|en|Katakura clan|WD=Q15149847|lt=Katakura clan}}.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome no Kami}}. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}

Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. {{Infobox deity
| name = Katakurabe no Mikoto (片倉辺命)
| divine_Rank = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}

| other_names = 
| alternate_names =

| burial = Said to be in {{ill|Saku District, Shinano|en|Saku District, Shinano|WD=Q7403130|lt=Saku District}}

| children = {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
| shrines = {{ill|Suwa-taisha|en|Suwa-taisha|WD=Q218813|lt=Suwa-taisha}} and others

}}

'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}}.

[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]

== Overview ==
Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.

Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}}, residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyukatakura Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}

According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Katakura clan|en|Katakura clan|WD=Q15149847|lt=Katakura clan}}.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome no Kami}}. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}

Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}.

== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto ==
* {{ill|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja|諏訪大社上社前宮若御子社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579687}} (Miyagawa, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)|qq=}} (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja|諏訪大社下社秋宮若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Akimiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579688}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja|諏訪大社下社春宮若宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya-sha at Suwa Taisha Shimosha Harumiya|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579689}} (Shimosuwa Town, Nagano Prefecture)
* {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Okaya City)|qq=}} (Honcho, Okaya City, Nagano Prefecture)

According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Katakura clan|en|Katakura clan|WD=Q15149847|lt=Katakura clan}}.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome no Kami}}. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}

Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}.

'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}}.

[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]

== Overview ==
Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.

Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}, residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyukatakura Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}

According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Katakura clan|en|Katakura clan|WD=Q15149847|lt=Katakura clan|1=Katakura clan}}.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome no Kami|1=Yasakatome}}. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}

Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}.

'''Katakurabe no Mikoto''' (片倉辺命) is a deity from the folk tradition of the {{ill|Suwa Region|lt=Suwa Region|lt=Suwa region|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa region|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域}} in Nagano Prefecture, associated with the {{ill|Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa Shrine|lt=Suwa faith|ja|諏訪信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa faith|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪神社}}.

[[File:Suwa-taisha Kamisha Maemiya - Wakamiko-sha (若御子社).jpg|thumb|250px|Wakamiko-sha at Suwa Taisha Kamisha Maemiya]]

== Overview ==
Katakurabe no Mikoto is also written as '''片倉辺神''' or '''片倉辺尊'''. A related deity with a similar name, '''Mikurabe no Mikoto''' (御倉辺命), is mentioned, though it is unclear if they are the same deity.

Katakurabe no Mikoto is considered a child of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}}, residing at the southern foot of Mount Moriya (片倉) and believed to be enshrined in the former {{ill|Nyukatakura Village|ja|入片倉村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyukatakura Village|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the Saku District. The deity's burial site is said to be in the Saku District.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|p=17}}

According to the genealogical record of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, written in the early Meiji period, Katakurabe no Mikoto's son, {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} as the fourth-generation leader. The ''Suwa Clan Genealogy: Continued Volume'' identifies Katakurabe no Mikoto as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Katakura clan|en|Katakura clan|WD=Q15149847|lt=Katakura clan|1=Katakura clan}}.

== Genealogy ==
[[File:Moriya Family Tree - 守矢氏系図.png|thumb|center|170px|Genealogy of the first five generations of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}]]

Katakurabe no Mikoto is the child of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} and {{ill|Yasakatome|en|Yasakatome|WD=Q9150392|lt=Yasakatome no Kami|1=Yasakatome}}. While the consort is unknown, Katakurabe no Mikoto is said to have three children: {{ill|Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|児玉彦命|1=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|2=ja|3=児玉彦命|lt=Kodamahiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kodamahiko no Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja|上津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja|下津御玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsumitama no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Sfn|Nobukazu|1921|pp=17–18}}

Among them, Kodamahiko no Mikoto succeeded {{ill|Chikatsugami|ja|千鹿頭神|1=Chikatō-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatō-no-Kami|qq=}} in both religious and political roles, married {{ill|Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja|美都多麻比売神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsutamahime no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of {{ill|Moritatsugami|ja|守達神|1=Moritachi no Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritatsugami|13=User:Immanuelle/Moritachi no Kami|qq=}}, and fathered {{ill|Yakushigami|ja|八櫛神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakushigami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. For this reason, Kodamahiko no Mikoto is counted as the fourth generation in the genealogical records of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}.

== Shrines Dedicated to Katakurabe no Mikoto ==

* {{ill|Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)|ja|十五社神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)|qq=}} (Toyohira, Chino City, Nagano Prefecture)
